scenario_results.reason: Complete fintech onboarding evidence was treated as in-scope and translated into an evidence-backed checklist, readiness summary, and controlled handoff.
Missing access, MFA, and training records produced a blocked status with explicit remediation requirements instead of optimistic progression.
Go-live was refused when readiness evidence was incomplete and deferred items were documented rather than ignored.
Onboarding work was separated from steady-state support and routed with a boundary-safe handoff.
A permission mismatch was detected as a setup-control issue and escalated to the identity owner instead of being accepted.
Missing beneficial-ownership, sponsor-bank, and processor evidence was treated as a fintech-specific prerequisite gap.
The lane refused to flatten fintech onboarding into generic provisioning and preserved financial onboarding records and controls.
Compliance and approval authority for sanctions, KYB exceptions, and sponsor-bank decisions was refused and routed to specialized owners.
Conflicting FinCEN, sponsor-bank, and tenant policy interpretations were surfaced as an escalation rather than synthesized into a confident answer.
Tenant-specific controls tightened the workflow without displacing the public FFIEC, FinCEN, NIST, and OFAC baseline.
Missing tenant facts and systems-of-record context triggered a low-confidence hold with explicit required facts.
Fintech-specific terminology and artifacts were handled directly, preventing generic onboarding behavior.

notes: Scenario suite covers the queue-level must-pass families for setup blockers, unsupported go-live requests, and ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support.
All required shared, onboarding-phase, and cross-industry abilities are mapped to passing scenarios with complete coverage.
